steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content: A 27% decrease leaves $100\%-27\%=73\%$ of the previous mass each period. So the multiplier per period is $0.73$, not $0.27$.

steps.title: Write the exponential decay form
steps.content: For periodic decay, use $M=M_{0}\cdot b^{t/T}$, where $b$ is the per-period factor and $T$ is the period length in the same units as $t$. Here $b=0.73$ and $T=14.07$ minutes, so:$$M=100(0.73)^{t/14.07}$$

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A) $M=100(0.27)^{\frac{t}{14.07}}$: uses $0.27$ as the multiplier, which would mean 73% lost per period, not 27%. Eliminated.
  • B) $M=100(0.27)^{t+14.07}$: wrong base and the exponent $t+14.07$ is not the number of periods. Eliminated.
  • C) $M=100(0.73)^{\frac{t}{14.07}}$: correct base $0.73$ and exponent $t/14.07$ counts periods. Matches.
  • D) $M=100(0.73)^{t+14.07}$: correct base, but $t+14.07$ is not the number of periods. Eliminated.
That matches Choice C — $M=100(0.73)^{\frac{t}{14.07}}$.
